import io
import string

fn is_digit(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b >= 48 and b <= 57
end

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
end

fn parse_two(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut nums = [0, 0]
  mut idx = 0
  while idx < 2
    while i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if is_ws(b)
          i = i + 1
        else
          i = n + 1
        end
      when None
        i = n + 1
      end
    end
    if i > n
      i = i - (n + 1) + n
    end
    mut sign = 1
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 45
        sign = -1
        i = i + 1
      else if b == 43
        i = i + 1
      else
        i = i
      end
    when None
      i = i
    end
    mut val = 0
    mut reading = true
    while reading
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if is_digit(b)
          val = val * 10 + (b - 48)
          i = i + 1
        else
          reading = false
        end
      when None
        reading = false
      end
    end
    val = val * sign
    if idx == 0
      nums = [val, 0]
    else
      let first = match nums.get(0)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        0
      end
      nums = [first, val]
    end
    idx = idx + 1
  end
  nums
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let input = io.read_to_end()
  let nums = parse_two(input)
  let a = match nums.get(0)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
  let b = match nums.get(1)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
  let r = a * a + b * b
  println("r=#{r}")
end
